title: ESTC Q3 Results: AI Adoption, Platform Consolidation Drive Growth
text: Elastic exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q3 2025, delivering revenue of $423.5 million, representing 15.9% year-over-year growth and surpassing analyst estimates by 1.3%. The company's adjusted earnings per share reached $0.64, beating consensus projections by 11.1%. The firm raised its full-year revenue guidance to $1.72 billion (up from $1.68 billion) and increased adjusted EPS guidance to $2.43, a 4.7% improvement from prior expectations. Operating margins stood at 16.5%, exceeding analyst estimates by 4.1%. Key Growth Drivers: AI Platform Momentum—over 2,450 customers now utilize Elastic Cloud for generative AI applications. Management emphasized that AI-driven solutions attracted new customers while expanding use cases among existing accounts, particularly benefiting from the platform's capability in handling unstructured data. Large Enterprise Deals—Elastic secured a record 30+ customer commitments exceeding $1 million in annual value, with five deals surpassing $10 million and two exceeding $20 million. Notably, the company won a significant contract with the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). Platform Consolidation—organizations accelerated migration from legacy systems to Elastic's unified platform, particularly in security and observability segments, reflecting customers' desire to streamline infrastructure and reduce operational costs. Outlook and Future Catalysts: Management highlighted disciplined sales execution as a competitive advantage and pointed to a healthy pipeline supporting continued momentum. The company will focus on expanding AI use cases, scaling enterprise relationships, and driving platform consolidation in upcoming quarters.
